reasoning: I will provide a concise summary of the article, capturing the key facts: the incident and casualties, ongoing investigations, statements from officials, and the traffic/policy updates.
output: Eleven people were killed when a vintage Hawker Hunter jet crashed into traffic on the A27 at Shoreham on 22 August, with recovery described as extremely challenging. Sussex Police have finished their work at the scene, but investigations continue, and the AAIB intends to interview 51-year-old pilot Andy Hill. The pilot’s family issued condolences; the initial AAIB report said he was thrown clear, though it remains unclear whether he initiated his ejection. West Sussex County Council said the westbound A27 lane would reopen next week, but roadside screens will stay up for land reclamation work.
